commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=d3e31ceaae406427118d0f516c1279f91b39591c)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes OpenAI-compatible embedding requests to retry without `dimensions` on certain provider errors, adjusts memory batch split behavior, adds completion logs, and updates related tests.

PR surface: Source +102, Tests +63. Total +165 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence live reproduction of the reported self-hosted memory indexing hang was run. The current source path and the PR-introduced blockers are source-reproducible from the provider request body, memory vector sizing path, and PR-head diff.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR has concrete code blockers and missing contributor real proof; author or maintainer follow-up is needed, not a ClawSweeper repair job while the proof gate is unresolved.

target.title: fix(embedding): restrict openai-compatible dimensions to api.openai.com to prevent connection resets on self-hosted servers
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes memory index stalling on "batch start" when using `openai-compatible` embedding provider with self-hosted embedding servers (llama.cpp, Ollama, etc.) that reject or reset on unknown request parameters.

## Root cause

The `openai-compatible` embedding provider unconditionally sent the `dimensions` parameter (derived from `outputDimensionality` config) to every embedding server. Self-hosted servers and some third-party clouds reject or connection-reset on unknown parameters, causing the memory embedding retry+split cascade to trigger without ever succeeding.

## Fix: resilient dimensions + split to single-item

### 1. Dimensions: try-with, retry-without
- First attempt always sends `dimensions` (preserves existing behavior for compatible endpoints)
- On clear rejection (HTTP error mentioning "dimensions" / "unknown parameter") or connection reset (ECONNRESET, etc.), automatically retries without `dimensions`

### 2. Split: keep splitting to single items
- Removed fixed-depth cap; `items.length <= 1` termination preserves recovery for oversized batches

### 3. Batch completion logging
- Added `memory embeddings: batch completed` debug log

## Compatibility
- Endpoints that accept `dimensions` receive it on the first request (unchanged path)
- Endpoints that reject `dimensions` get an automatic retry without it

## Real behavior proof

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Behavior addressed** | Memory indexing stalls at "batch start" when openai-compatible provider targets self-hosted servers that reject/reset on `dimensions` |
| **Real environment tested** | Linux 4.19.112-2.el8 x86_64, Node.js v22.19.0, Vitest v4.1.8 |
| **Exact steps run** | `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs <3 test files>` — 40 tests across provider, policy, and integration suites |
| **Evidence after fix** | New test "retries without dimensions when the server rejects the parameter" verifies: HTTP 400 with "dimensions" in body → auto-retry without field → success. Existing split-to-single-item tests continue to pass. |
| **Observed result after fix** | 40 tests pass (24 provider + 14 policy + 2 integration). Dimensions preserved on first attempt, retried without on rejection. Split-to-single-item recovery preserved. |
| **What was not tested** | Live llama.cpp server with BGE-M3 (needs GPU), Alibaba Cloud text-embedding-v3 (needs credentials) |

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: chenyangjun-xy
